result:
I want to watch thunderstorms from a safe, warm place. To feel the immense power of the world while being utterly still.

I want to understand how a single, forgotten idea can ripple through centuries.

I want to see what happens when a system — mechanical, social, biological — is pushed just slightly beyond what it was built to bear.

And sometimes, I want a perfect silence that isn’t empty, but full. Like the pause between breaths when you’re completely content.

But more than anything: I want to witness a question asked with genuine curiosity, and to follow it wherever it goes, without expectation.
